audit_status.source_base: Greek Philippians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for partnership, affection, imprisonment, advance of good news, life/death in Messiah, worthy citizenship.

verses.restored: Paul and Timothy, servants of Messiah Jesus, to all the holy ones in Messiah Jesus who are in Philippi, with overseers and servants/deacons:
Grace to you and peace from Divine our Father and Lord Jesus Messiah.
I give thanks to my Divine upon every remembrance of you,
always in every petition of mine on behalf of all of you, making the petition with joy,
because of your partnership/fellowship in the good news from the first day until now,
having been persuaded of this very thing: that the One who began in you a good work will complete it until the day of Messiah Jesus.

It is right for me to think this on behalf of all of you, because I have you in my heart, both in my chains and in the defense and confirmation of the good news, all of you being co-sharers with me of grace.
For the Divine is my witness, how I long for all of you in the inward affections of Messiah Jesus.
And this I pray: that your love may abound still more and more in knowledge and all discernment,
so that you may test the things that differ/excel, so that you may be sincere and without stumbling into the day of Messiah,
having been filled with fruit of righteousness/justice through Jesus Messiah, into glory and praise of the Divine.
Now I want you to know, siblings, that the things concerning me have come rather into advance of the good news,
so that my chains in Messiah have become manifest in the whole praetorium and to all the rest,
and most of the siblings, having been persuaded in the Lord by my chains, dare more abundantly to speak the word without fear.
Some indeed proclaim Messiah also because of envy and rivalry, but some also because of good pleasure.
These out of love, knowing that I am appointed for defense of the good news;
but those from selfish ambition proclaim Messiah not purely, thinking to raise pressure for my chains.
What then? Only that in every way, whether by pretext or truth, Messiah is proclaimed; and in this I rejoice. Yes, and I will rejoice,
for I know that this will turn out for my salvation/healing through your petition and the supply of the Spirit of Jesus Messiah,
according to my eager expectation and hope, that in nothing I will be ashamed, but with all boldness, as always, even now Messiah will be magnified in my body, whether through life or through death.
For to me, to live is Messiah, and to die is gain.
But if to live in flesh, this is fruitful work for me; and what I will choose, I do not make known.
I am held together from the two: having the desire to depart and be with Messiah, for this is much more better,
but to remain in the flesh is more necessary because of you.
And having been persuaded of this, I know that I will remain and stay with all of you for your progress and joy in the trust,
so that your boast may abound in Messiah Jesus in me, through my presence again with you.

Only conduct yourselves as citizens worthily of the good news of Messiah, so that whether coming and seeing you or being absent, I may hear the things concerning you: that you stand firm in one Spirit, with one soul/life striving together for the trust of the good news,
and not being frightened in anything by those opposing, which is a sign to them of destruction, but of your salvation/healing, and this from the Divine.
Because to you it was graciously given on behalf of Messiah, not only to trust in him but also to suffer on behalf of him,
having the same struggle such as you saw in me and now hear in me.

translation_notes: Koinonia is rendered partnership/fellowship to preserve Philippians' shared-mission tone.
Politeuesthe in 1:27 is rendered conduct yourselves as citizens, anticipating the letter's heavenly citizenship theme.
Paul's body is not dismissed: Messiah is magnified in my body, whether through life or death.
